rawText:
I am honored to be with you today at your commencement from one of the finest universities in the world. I never graduated from college. Truth be told, this is the closest I've ever gotten to a college graduation. Today I want to tell you three stories from my life. That's it. No big deal. Just three stories.

The first story is about connecting the dots.

I dropped out of Reed College after the first 6 months, but then stayed around as a drop-in for another 18 months or so before I really quit. So why did I drop out?

It started before I was born. My biological mother was a young, unwed college graduate student, and she decided to put me up for adoption. She felt very strongly that I should be adopted by college graduates, so everything was all set for me to be adopted at birth by a lawyer and his wife. Except that when I popped out they decided at the last minute that they really wanted a girl.

So my parents, who were on a waiting list, got a call in the middle of the night asking: "We have an unexpected baby boy; do you want him?" They said: "Of course." My biological mother later found out that my mother had never graduated from college and that my father had never graduated from high school. She refused to sign the final adoption papers. She only relented a few months later when my parents promised that I would someday go to college.

And 17 years later I did go to college. But I naively chose a college that was almost as expensive as Stanford, and all of my working-class parents' savings were being spent on my college tuition. After six months, I couldn't see the value in it. I had no idea what I wanted to do with my life and no idea how college was going to help me figure it out.

And here I was spending all of the money my parents had saved their entire life. So I decided to drop out and trust that it would all work out OK. It was pretty scary at the time, but looking back it was one of the best decisions I ever made. The minute I dropped out I could stop taking the required classes that didn't interest me, and begin dropping in on the ones that looked interesting.

It wasn't all romantic. I didn't have a dorm room, so I slept on the floor in friends' rooms, I returned coke bottles for the 5 cent deposits to buy food with, and I would walk the 7 miles across town every Sunday night to get one good meal a week at the Hare Krishna temple. I loved it.

overview.title: Steve Jobs' Stanford University Commencement Speech

sentences.original: I am honored to be with you today at your commencement from one of the finest universities in the world.
sentences.translation: 세계 최고의 대학 중 하나인 이곳에서 여러분의 졸업식에 함께할 수 있어 영광입니다.

grammarElements.text: from one of the finest universities in the world
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (출처)

vocabulary.example: I am honored to receive this award.
The commencement ceremony was held in May.
This is one of the finest restaurants in the city.

patterns.explanation: ~하게 되어 영광이라는 격식적 인사 표현. 연설 시작에 자주 사용된다.
examples: I am honored to speak before you today.
I am honored to accept this invitation.

drillQuestions.question: I am _____ to be with you today.
Stanford is one of the _____ universities in the world.

sentences.notes: 연설의 전형적인 시작 — 청중에 대한 존경을 표현한다. commencement는 "시작"이라는 뜻도 있지만, 미국 대학에서는 "졸업식"을 의미한다.

koreanAlignment.en: from one of the finest universities

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 겸손(captatio benevolentiae) — 청중에게 경의를 표하며 시작하는 전통적 연설 기법

sentences.original: I never graduated from college.
sentences.translation: 저는 대학을 졸업한 적이 없습니다.

vocabulary.example: She graduated from Harvard in 2020.

patterns.explanation: 과거에 한 번도 ~한 적이 없다는 표현. never + 과거 동사로 경험의 부재를 나타낸다.
examples: I never expected this to happen.
I never thought I would be here.

drillQuestions.question: I never _____ from college.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장의 격식적 칭찬 직후 이 짧고 솔직한 고백이 나와 극적인 대비를 만든다. 청중(스탠퍼드 졸업생)에게 충격적인 발언으로 관심을 사로잡는 기법이다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반전(anticlimax) — 격식적 시작 직후의 솔직한 고백으로 극적 효과를 만듦

sentences.original: Truth be told, this is the closest I've ever gotten to a college graduation.
sentences.translation: 사실을 말하자면, 이것이 제가 대학 졸업에 가장 가까이 다가간 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: the closest I've ever gotten to a college graduation
grammarElements.label: 보어 (최상급 + 현재완료)

vocabulary.example: The truth is sometimes hard to hear.

expressions.usage: 솔직한 고백을 할 때 사용하는 관용 표현

expressions.phrase: the closest I've ever gotten to

patterns.pattern: This is the [superlative] I've ever [past participle]
patterns.explanation: 내 인생에서 가장 ~한 것이라는 경험 최상급 표현
examples: This is the best meal I've ever had.
This is the hardest test I've ever taken.

drillQuestions.question: This is the closest I've _____ gotten to a graduation.

sentences.notes: 유머를 통해 청중과의 거리를 좁히는 기법이다. "truth be told"는 가정법 현재(subjunctive)의 잔재로, 문법적으로 "if truth be told"에서 if가 생략된 형태이다.
pronunciationNotes: gotten: /ˈɡɑːtn/ — '가튼', 미국식 영어에서 get의 과거분사. 영국식은 got을 주로 사용
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 자기비하적 유머(self-deprecating humor) — 졸업식에서 졸업 못한 사람이라는 아이러니

sentences.original: Today I want to tell you three stories from my life.
sentences.translation: 오늘 저는 제 인생에서 세 가지 이야기를 들려드리고 싶습니다.

vocabulary.example: Let me tell you a story.

patterns.pattern: I want to tell you [number] stories from [source]
patterns.explanation: 이야기의 수와 출처를 미리 예고하는 연설 도입부 패턴
examples: I want to tell you two lessons from my experience.
I want to share three ideas from our research.

drillQuestions.question: Today I want to _____ you three stories.

sentences.notes: 연설의 구조를 미리 예고하는 "로드맵(roadmap)" 기법이다. 청중에게 "세 가지 이야기"라고 미리 알려줌으로써 기대감과 구조적 명확성을 제공한다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 미리보기(foreshadowing) — 연설 전체 구조를 세 가지 이야기로 예고

sentences.original: That's it. No big deal. Just three stories.
sentences.translation: 그게 전부입니다. 별거 아닙니다. 그냥 세 가지 이야기입니다.

vocabulary.example: It's no big deal, don't worry about it.

patterns.explanation: 짧은 불완전 문장을 연속으로 사용해 대화적이고 친근한 느낌을 주는 패턴
examples: Here's my plan. Simple. Just one step.
That's all. Nothing fancy. Just the truth.

drillQuestions.question: That's it. No _____ deal. Just three stories.

sentences.notes: 세 개의 짧은 문장(문장 파편)을 연속으로 사용하여 친근하고 대화적인 톤을 만든다. "No big deal"과 "Just"는 겸손함을 표현하면서 동시에 이야기에 대한 기대를 낮추는 역설적 효과를 만든다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 의도적 겸양(litotes) — 위대한 연설을 "별거 아닌 것"처럼 소개하여 친밀감을 형성

sentences.original: The first story is about connecting the dots.
sentences.translation: 첫 번째 이야기는 점들을 연결하는 것에 대한 것입니다.

vocabulary.example: Connecting with people is important.
The child drew dots on the paper.

expressions.usage: 과거의 경험들이 결국 연결됨을 비유적으로 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 순서대로 이야기의 주제를 소개하는 연설 전환 패턴
examples: The second lesson is about taking risks.
The first chapter is about building habits.

drillQuestions.question: The first story is about _____ the dots.

sentences.notes: "connecting the dots"는 잡스 연설의 핵심 은유이다. 어린이 퍼즐에서 점을 연결하면 그림이 완성되듯, 인생의 경험들도 나중에 보면 하나의 큰 그림으로 연결된다는 의미이다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 은유(metaphor) — 인생 경험을 "점들"에 비유하여 핵심 메시지를 전달

sentences.original: I dropped out of Reed College after the first 6 months, but then stayed around as a drop-in for another 18 months or so before I really quit.
sentences.translation: 저는 처음 6개월 후에 리드 대학을 중퇴했지만, 정말로 그만두기 전에 18개월 정도 더 비정규 청강생으로 머물렀습니다.

grammarElements.text: but then stayed around as a drop-in for another 18 months or so
grammarElements.label: 대조절

vocabulary.example: He dropped out of school to start a business.
Drop-in students can attend lectures without registering.
She quit her job to travel the world.

expressions.usage: 완전히 떠나지 않고 근처에 남아 있었을 때

patterns.explanation: 시간 순서에 따라 세 가지 동작을 연결하는 서사적 패턴
examples: I left the company but then freelanced for a year before I started my own business.

drillQuestions.question: I dropped _____ of college after six months.

sentences.notes: "drop out"(중퇴)과 "drop-in"(청강)의 대비가 재치 있다. 같은 단어 "drop"을 다른 의미로 사용하는 말장난(wordplay)이다.

sentences.original: So why did I drop out?
sentences.translation: 그래서 왜 중퇴했을까요?

vocabulary.example: Why did you drop out of the program?

patterns.explanation: 자문자답(rhetorical question) 패턴. 스스로 질문을 던지고 바로 답하는 연설 기법.
examples: So why did I leave? Let me explain.
So why did I start over? Here is the reason.

drillQuestions.question: So why _____ I drop out?

sentences.notes: 자문자답(self-directed question)은 청중의 궁금증을 대변하며 자연스럽게 이야기를 이끌어가는 효과적인 연설 기법이다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) — 청중의 궁금증을 대변하여 이야기의 흐름을 이어감

sentences.original: It started before I was born.
sentences.translation: 그것은 제가 태어나기 전에 시작되었습니다.

vocabulary.example: I was born in 1995.

patterns.explanation: 이야기의 시작점이 특정 과거 사건 이전임을 나타내는 패턴
examples: It started before the company was founded.
It started before anyone realized.

drillQuestions.question: It started _____ I was born.

sentences.notes: 매우 짧지만 극적인 문장. 자신의 입양 이야기를 출생 이전부터 시작함으로써 이야기의 깊이를 더한다. "It"은 앞서 질문한 "왜 중퇴했는가"의 이유를 가리킨다.

sentences.original: My biological mother was a young, unwed college graduate student, and she decided to put me up for adoption.
sentences.translation: 제 생모는 젊고 미혼인 대학원생이었고, 저를 입양 보내기로 결정했습니다.

grammarElements.text: a young, unwed college graduate student
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: and she decided to put me up for adoption
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: She wanted to find her biological parents.
Unwed mothers faced social stigma in the past.
The couple considered adoption.

expressions.usage: 아이를 입양 보내기로 결정했음을 나타낼 때
양부모와 구분하여 혈연 부모를 지칭할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] decided to put [someone] up for adoption
patterns.explanation: 누군가를 입양 보내기로 결정했다는 표현. put up for는 "~에 내놓다"의 의미.
examples: The young couple decided to put the baby up for adoption.

drillQuestions.question: She decided to put me up _____ adoption.

sentences.notes: 입양이라는 개인적이고 민감한 주제를 담담하게 이야기한다. "put up for adoption"은 입양 관련 핵심 관용 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: was a young, unwed college graduate student

sentences.original: She felt very strongly that I should be adopted by college graduates, so everything was all set for me to be adopted at birth by a lawyer and his wife.
sentences.translation: 그녀는 제가 반드시 대학 졸업자에게 입양되어야 한다고 매우 강하게 느꼈고, 그래서 제가 태어날 때 한 변호사와 그의 아내에게 입양될 수 있도록 모든 것이 준비되어 있었습니다.

grammarElements.text: that I should be adopted by college graduates
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: so everything was all set for me to be adopted at birth by a lawyer and his wife
grammarElements.label: 결과절

vocabulary.example: She strongly believes in education.
They decided to adopt a child.
Everything is all set for the party.

patterns.explanation: 강한 신념과 그에 따른 결과를 연결하는 패턴
examples: He felt strongly that everyone deserved a chance, so he started a scholarship fund.

drillQuestions.question: She felt strongly _____ I should be adopted by graduates.

sentences.notes: "should be adopted by college graduates"라는 조건이 잡스 인생 이야기의 아이러니를 만든다 — 생모는 대졸자를 원했지만, 실제 양부모는 대학 졸업자가 아니었다.

koreanAlignment.en: for me to be adopted at birth
koreanAlignment.ko: 제가 태어날 때 입양될 수 있도록

koreanAlignment.en: by a lawyer and his wife

sentences.original: Except that when I popped out they decided at the last minute that they really wanted a girl.
sentences.translation: 그런데 제가 태어났을 때 그들은 막판에 정말로 여자아이를 원한다고 결정했습니다.

grammarElements.text: that they really wanted a girl
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: The baby popped out healthy and strong.
He changed his mind at the last minute.

patterns.explanation: 예상과 다른 전개를 "except that"으로 도입하는 서사적 전환 패턴
examples: Except that when the day came, she decided she was not ready.

drillQuestions.question: They decided at the _____ minute that they wanted a girl.

sentences.notes: "popped out"은 출산을 매우 구어적이고 유머러스하게 표현한 것이다. 격식적인 연설에서 이런 구어 표현을 사용하면 청중과의 거리를 좁히는 효과가 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: that they really wanted a girl

pronunciationNotes: minute: /ˈmɪnɪt/ — "미닛", 시간 단위. 형용사 minute(/maɪˈnjuːt/)와 구별

sentences.original: So my parents, who were on a waiting list, got a call in the middle of the night asking: "We have an unexpected baby boy; do you want him?"
sentences.translation: 그래서 대기 명단에 있던 저의 부모님은 한밤중에 전화를 받았습니다: "예상치 못한 남자아이가 있는데, 원하십니까?"

grammarElements.text: who were on a waiting list
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (부연 설명)

grammarElements.text: in the middle of the night
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구

grammarElements.text: asking: "We have an unexpected baby boy; do you want him?"
grammarElements.label: 현재분사구 (내용)

vocabulary.example: We are on the waiting list for the restaurant.
It was an unexpected surprise.

expressions.phrase: in the middle of the night
expressions.usage: 늦은 밤 시간을 극적으로 표현할 때
전화 연락을 받은 것을 구어적으로 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 삽입 관계절로 상황을 설명하고, 분사구문으로 내용을 전달하는 서사적 패턴
examples: The students, who were waiting outside, got a message saying the test was cancelled.

drillQuestions.question: They got a call in the _____ of the night.

sentences.notes: 극적인 장면 묘사이다. "한밤중의 전화"라는 설정이 영화 같은 서사를 만든다. 직접 인용으로 대화를 생생하게 재현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: who were on a waiting list
in the middle of the night
We have an unexpected baby boy

sentences.original: They said: "Of course."
sentences.translation: 그들은 "물론이지요"라고 답했습니다.

vocabulary.example: Of course, you can come to the party.

patterns.explanation: 짧고 강렬한 대답을 직접 인용하여 감동적 순간을 만드는 패턴
examples: She said: "Yes." He said: "Always."

drillQuestions.question: They said: "_____ course."

sentences.notes: 이 짧은 문장이 감동적인 이유는 "예상치 못한 아이를 원하느냐"는 질문에 대한 즉각적이고 확고한 긍정 답변이기 때문이다. 단순함이 곧 힘이다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 간결함(brevity) — 짧은 답변으로 양부모의 따뜻한 마음을 극적으로 전달

sentences.original: My biological mother later found out that my mother had never graduated from college and that my father had never graduated from high school.
sentences.translation: 제 생모는 나중에 제 어머니가 대학을 졸업한 적이 없고 제 아버지가 고등학교를 졸업한 적이 없다는 것을 알게 되었습니다.

grammarElements.text: that my mother had never graduated from college
grammarElements.label: 명사절 1 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: and that my father had never graduated from high school
grammarElements.label: 명사절 2 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: I found out the truth about the situation.

patterns.explanation: 두 가지 사실을 동시에 발견했음을 병렬 구조로 나타내는 패턴
examples: She found out that the store was closed and that the owner had moved away.

drillQuestions.question: She found out that my mother _____ never graduated.

sentences.notes: "had never graduated"는 대과거(past perfect)로, "found out"보다 더 이전의 사실을 나타낸다. "my mother" vs "my biological mother"의 구분이 중요하다.

koreanAlignment.en: that my mother had never graduated from college
koreanAlignment.ko: 제 어머니가 대학을 졸업한 적이 없고

koreanAlignment.en: and that my father had never graduated from high school
koreanAlignment.ko: 제 아버지가 고등학교를 졸업한 적이 없다는 것을

sentences.original: She refused to sign the final adoption papers.
sentences.translation: 그녀는 최종 입양 서류에 서명하기를 거부했습니다.

vocabulary.example: He refused to answer the question.
Please sign the document here.

expressions.usage: 공식 문서에 동의하지 않음을 나타낼 때

patterns.explanation: 주어가 ~하기를 거부했다는 패턴. refuse는 to부정사만 취한다(동명사 불가).
examples: The witness refused to testify.
The employee refused to work overtime.

drillQuestions.question: She refused _____ sign the papers.

sentences.notes: refuse는 반드시 to부정사와 함께 쓴다(refuse to do). refuse + 동명사(refuse doing)는 불가능하다는 점에 주의.

sentences.original: She only relented a few months later when my parents promised that I would someday go to college.
sentences.translation: 부모님이 제가 언젠가 대학에 보내겠다고 약속했을 때, 그녀는 몇 달 후에야 비로소 마음을 바꾸었습니다.

grammarElements.text: when my parents promised that I would someday go to college
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 (조건)

vocabulary.example: After hours of arguing, she finally relented.
He promised to help me with the project.
I hope to visit Japan someday.

expressions.usage: 오랜 거부 끝에 마침내 양보했을 때

patterns.explanation: 특정 조건이 충족되어야만 행동이 이루어졌다는 패턴
examples: He only agreed when they offered more money.
She only smiled when she heard the good news.

drillQuestions.question: She only _____ when my parents made a promise.

sentences.notes: relent는 "강경한 태도를 누그러뜨리다"라는 뜻의 고급 어휘이다. 이 문장은 잡스의 대학 진학 약속이 입양의 조건이었음을 보여주며, 이후 그가 대학을 중퇴하는 아이러니로 이어진다.

koreanAlignment.en: that I would someday go to college

sentences.original: And 17 years later I did go to college.
sentences.translation: 그리고 17년 후, 저는 정말로 대학에 갔습니다.

vocabulary.example: Two years later, they got married.

expressions.usage: do/does/did를 동사 앞에 써서 강조할 때

patterns.explanation: "did + 동사원형"은 강조의 do로, "정말로 ~했다"는 의미를 더한다.
examples: And five years later, I did return to my hometown.
And eventually, she did find her answer.

drillQuestions.question: And 17 years later I _____ go to college. (강조)

sentences.notes: "did go"에서 did는 강조의 조동사이다. 생모와의 약속("언젠가 대학에 보내겠다")을 17년 후 이행했음을 강조하는 효과가 있다.
pronunciationNotes: did: /dɪd/ — 강조할 때는 "디~드"로 약간 길게 발음

sentences.original: But I naively chose a college that was almost as expensive as Stanford, and all of my working-class parents' savings were being spent on my college tuition.
sentences.translation: 하지만 저는 순진하게도 스탠퍼드만큼 비싼 대학을 선택했고, 노동자 계급인 부모님의 모든 저축이 제 대학 등록금에 쓰이고 있었습니다.

grammarElements.text: a college that was almost as expensive as Stanford
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (관계절 포함)

grammarElements.text: and all of my working-class parents' savings were being spent on my college tuition
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (수동태 과거진행)

vocabulary.example: I naively believed everything he said.
He grew up in a working-class family.
College tuition has increased dramatically.
They used their life savings to buy a house.

expressions.usage: 과거 진행 수동태로 지속적 상황을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 과거의 순진한 선택과 그 결과를 설명하는 회고적 패턴
examples: I naively accepted a job that was far beyond my abilities.

drillQuestions.question: The college was almost as _____ as Stanford.

sentences.notes: "naively"는 과거의 자신을 솔직하게 반성하는 어조이다. "were being spent"는 과거진행 수동태로, 저축이 계속 소진되고 있었던 상황을 생생하게 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a college that was almost as expensive as Stanford
and all of my working-class parents' savings

sentences.original: So I decided to drop out and trust that it would all work out OK.
sentences.translation: 그래서 저는 중퇴하고 모든 것이 잘 될 것이라고 믿기로 결정했습니다.

grammarElements.text: and trust that it would all work out OK
grammarElements.label: 병렬 동사 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: You have to trust the process.
Don't worry, everything will work out.

expressions.usage: 상황이 결국 좋게 해결될 것이라는 낙관적 표현
확신 없는 상황에서 믿음으로 결단할 때

patterns.pattern: I decided to [action] and trust that [hopeful outcome]
patterns.explanation: 결단과 믿음을 결합하는 패턴. 불확실한 상황에서의 용기를 표현.
examples: She decided to quit and trust that she would find something better.

drillQuestions.question: I decided to drop out and _____ that it would work out.

sentences.notes: "trust that it would all work out"는 잡스의 핵심 철학 — 불확실한 미래에 대한 신뢰 — 을 요약한다. 이 메시지는 연설 전체를 관통한다.

koreanAlignment.en: So I decided to drop out
that it would all work out OK

sentences.original: It was pretty scary at the time, but looking back it was one of the best decisions I ever made.
sentences.translation: 당시에는 꽤 무서웠지만, 돌이켜보면 제가 한 최고의 결정 중 하나였습니다.

grammarElements.text: it was one of the best decisions I ever made
grammarElements.label: 대조절

vocabulary.example: Starting a new job can be scary.
It was a difficult decision to make.

expressions.phrase: one of the best ... I ever made

patterns.pattern: It was [adjective] at the time, but looking back it was [positive evaluation]
patterns.explanation: 당시의 감정과 현재 회고적 평가를 대비하는 패턴
examples: It was terrifying at the time, but looking back it was the right choice.

drillQuestions.question: It was scary at the time, but _____ back it was a great decision.

sentences.notes: 과거의 두려움과 현재의 긍정적 평가를 대비하는 전형적인 회고 구조이다. "pretty"는 구어에서 "꽤, 상당히"라는 부사로 자주 쓰인다.

koreanAlignment.en: it was one of the best decisions

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조(antithesis) — "scary at the time" vs "best decisions" — 과거와 현재 시점의 평가를 대비

sentences.original: It wasn't all romantic.
sentences.translation: 전부 낭만적이지는 않았습니다.

vocabulary.example: The idea of living abroad sounds romantic.

patterns.explanation: "전부 ~한 것은 아니었다"는 현실적 인정. 앞의 낙관적 이야기를 균형 잡는 역할.
examples: It wasn't all easy.
It wasn't all fun and games.

drillQuestions.question: It wasn't _____ romantic.

sentences.notes: 짧지만 전환의 역할을 하는 문장. 중퇴 후의 삶이 이상적이지만은 않았음을 솔직하게 인정하며, 이어지는 고난의 이야기로 넘어간다.

sentences.original: I didn't have a dorm room, so I slept on the floor in friends' rooms, I returned coke bottles for the 5 cent deposits to buy food with, and I would walk the 7 miles across town every Sunday night to get one good meal a week at the Hare Krishna temple.
sentences.translation: 저는 기숙사 방이 없어서 친구들 방 바닥에서 잤고, 음식을 사기 위해 콜라병을 반납해 5센트 보증금을 받았으며, 매주 일요일 밤 7마일을 걸어 도시를 횡단해 하레 크리슈나 사원에서 일주일에 한 끼 좋은 식사를 했습니다.

grammarElements.text: so I slept on the floor in friends' rooms
grammarElements.label: 결과절

grammarElements.text: I returned coke bottles for the 5 cent deposits to buy food with
grammarElements.label: 등위절

grammarElements.text: and I would walk the 7 miles across town every Sunday night to get one good meal a week at the Hare Krishna temple
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (과거 반복)

vocabulary.example: College students share dorm rooms.
You get the deposit back when you return the bottle.
We visited a Buddhist temple in Kyoto.

expressions.usage: 병 반납 보증금 제도를 설명할 때
과거의 반복적 행동을 회상할 때. would = used to와 유사

patterns.pattern: [didn't have X], so [improvisation A], [improvisation B], and [improvisation C]
patterns.explanation: 부족한 상황에서 여러 가지 임기응변을 나열하는 서사적 패턴
examples: We didn't have money, so we borrowed books, shared meals, and walked everywhere.

drillQuestions.question: I would walk 7 miles _____ town every Sunday.

sentences.notes: 잡스의 가난한 시절을 생생하게 묘사하는 긴 문장이다. "would walk"의 would는 과거 반복 행동을 나타낸다. 세 가지 고난(바닥 잠, 병 반납, 7마일 걷기)을 병렬로 나열하여 리듬감을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: I didn't have a dorm room
so I slept on the floor in friends' rooms
I returned coke bottles for the 5 cent deposits
and I would walk the 7 miles across town
to get one good meal a week

pronunciationNotes: Hare Krishna: /ˈhɑːreɪ ˈkrɪʃnə/ — "하레이 크리슈나", 힌두교 관련 이름
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삼중 나열(tricolon) — 세 가지 고난을 나열하여 어려웠던 시절을 강조

sentences.original: I loved it.
sentences.translation: 저는 그것을 사랑했습니다.

vocabulary.example: I love learning new things.

patterns.explanation: 짧은 문장으로 앞의 긴 고난 서술에 대한 긍정적 반응을 전달하는 패턴
examples: The training was brutal. I loved it.
The work was endless. I loved it.

drillQuestions.question: Despite all the hardships, I _____ it.

sentences.notes: 앞의 긴 고난 묘사 직후 "I loved it"이라는 짧은 문장이 나오는 대비가 강렬하다. 바닥에서 자고, 병을 모으고, 7마일을 걸었지만 그것을 "사랑했다"는 역설이 잡스의 낙관적 성격을 보여준다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역설(paradox) — 고난 속에서도 사랑했다는 역설적 진술로 메시지를 강화
